mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off behavior that drives S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and GDP growth signal a contraction in real economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of periods where both INDPRO and GDPC1 decline.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in residential construction permits signals weakening demand for housing, which correlates with negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST falls below 2000.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the CPI inflation rate above 2% signals central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and drives S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in nonfarm payrolls above 150,000 jobs signals strong labor market dynamics, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST decline.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals a supply-side shock, which drives S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average.
